title: Wild Golden World
subtitle: A First Look at Savanna Animals
author: Hannah Whitfield
subject: savanna animals and the wide golden places they live
flux_style: Soft hand-painted watercolour storybook illustration in an adorable, sweet kawaii style — large friendly sparkly eyes, a gentle happy face, soft edges and tender huggable charm — with every animal drawn in its OWN correct natural body shape and true proportions for its real species (flat animals stay flat, long animals stay long, tall animals stay tall, never forced into a uniform round chubby ball), loose simplified naive childlike style, gentle softly muted warm savanna palette of soft golds, warm ochres, dusty sage greens and warm honey sunlight, soft tender pastel watercolour washes, gentle visible brushwork, soft edges, painterly flat shapes, bright friendly, children's picture book art, not photorealistic, not a photograph, no text

topics: a friendly lion cub with a soft golden coat sitting in the tall dry grass of the sunny savanna
a gentle elephant calf with one long trunk and two big round ears standing beside a calm watering hole
a tall giraffe with a very long neck and warm brown patch markings nibbling leaves at the top of a flat-topped acacia tree
a plump zebra with bold black-and-white stripes grazing in the golden savanna grass
a big round hippo resting happily in the cool shallow water of a muddy river pool
a sturdy rhinoceros with a tall curved horn on its nose and a smaller second horn just behind it walking across the wide dusty plain
a sleek spotted cheetah with small round black spots and dark tear-line face markings resting on a low grassy mound
a spotted leopard lounging along a wide shady branch of a big savanna tree
a little meerkat with a thin smooth sandy-tan tail standing tall and upright on its back legs on a sandy mound, keeping watch over the plain
a cheerful warthog with two small curved tusks trotting through the dry golden grass
a tall ostrich with a long bare neck, a big fluffy round body of soft feathers and long strong legs standing on the open plain
a pink flamingo with long thin legs and a gracefully curved neck wading in a shallow blue-green lake
a graceful gazelle with slender gently curved horns standing alert in the tall golden grass
a playful vervet monkey with a soft grey coat and a long tail sitting on the branch of an acacia tree
a big African buffalo with wide curved horns standing calmly in the tall savanna grass
a little bat-eared fox with a soft sandy-grey coat and two very large upright rounded ears sitting calmly on the warm open savanna
a bright lilac-breasted roller bird with a soft lilac chest and sky-blue wing feathers perched on a bare branch
a grey crowned crane with a fan of stiff golden feathers on top of its head standing gracefully at the edge of a marshy pool
a shaggy wildebeest with smooth curved horns and a long gentle face grazing on the wide open plain
a leopard tortoise with a tall patterned domed shell walking slowly through the short dry grass
art_prompt: a soft watercolour storybook front cover: ONE single adorable, joyful lion cub as the BIG central hero of the cover, large and prominent and filling most of the frame, centred in the composition and taking up the majority of the picture, smiling a big warm happy smile with large sparkly friendly eyes and soft rosy cheeks, a soft fluffy golden coat and a little tufted tail, sitting cheerfully in the warm golden grass of a sunny savanna; soft tall golden grass, one distant flat-topped acacia tree and a warm honey-coloured sky, the background richly and evenly painted from top to bottom all the way to every edge with soft golds and warm light (no other animals, no people); the cub's head, ears, back and body are completely smooth, clean and bare with nothing sprouting from them; the cub looks happy, warm, gentle, sweet and inviting (a cheerful friendly smile, definitely NOT sad, NOT scary, NOT angry, NOT sleepy); exaggerated kawaii cuteness, the whole frame richly painted in gentle softly muted golds and warm light, bright cheerful and inviting, no washed-out or blank empty areas, no text, no words
blurb: Step into a wild golden world! From a fluffy lion cub in the tall sunny grass to a leopard tortoise plodding across the plain, this gentle first safari book takes early readers out onto the savanna to meet twenty amazing animals in the wide golden places they call home. Soft, glowing watercolours and simple, read-aloud rhymes make every page a warm discovery — a perfect first safari adventure for curious little ones.
